symptom: a dull ache in one shin that has been building for weeks with no injury to explain it
hook: one stretch of bone was blown outward into a thin-walled balloon far wider than the bone beside it
whatYouSee: One section of bone blown outward into a thin-walled balloon shape far wider than the bone beside it.
whyItMatters: Blood-filled cavities have expanded inside the bone and stretched its shell outward faster than the bone can reinforce it.
treatment: Surgeons scrape out the cyst and pack the space with bone graft and injections can help close it.
takeaway: A bone can blow up like a balloon and still hold its shape.

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with a dull ache in one shin that has been building for weeks with no injury to explain it.

Then the X-ray loaded 😭

And one stretch of bone was blown outward into a thin-walled balloon far wider than the bone beside it.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Aneurysmal bone cyst

👀 What you see:
One section of bone blown outward into a thin-walled balloon shape far wider than the bone beside it.

🦴 Why it matters:
Blood-filled cavities have expanded inside the bone and stretched its shell outward faster than the bone can reinforce it.

💊 Treatment:

Surgeons scrape out the cyst and pack the space with bone graft and injections can help close it.
generated.ctaText:
If these weird X-rays keep pulling you in.

I put 5 of the strangest into a free pack.

Guess hopital then flip for what each one really is.

Grab it free.

laypersonQuestion: Does that bone look like it swallowed a balloon?
seedHint: Look at where the bone suddenly widens and ask yourself if bone is supposed to do that.
